full_response:
I need to find the matrix product [N]·[M] using summation notation.

For matrix multiplication, when we multiply two matrices [N] and [M], the element in the i-th row and j-th column of the product matrix is obtained by taking the dot product of the i-th row of [N] with the j-th column of [M].

Let me denote the product matrix as [P] = [N]·[M].

Then each element P_{ij} of the product matrix is given by:

P_{ij} = (i-th row of N) · (j-th column of M)

Using summation notation, this can be written as:

P_{ij} = Σ_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik} M_{kj}

This means:
- We sum over the index k from 1 to 3
- For each k, we multiply the element from the i-th row and k-th column of [N] with the element from the k-th row and j-th column of [M]
- We add all these products together

So the complete matrix product [N]·[M] in summation notation is:

[N]·[M] = [P] where P_{ij} = Σ_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik} M_{kj} for i,j ∈ {1,2,3}

This can also be written in more compact notation as:
([N]·[M])_{ij} = Σ_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik} M_{kj}

\boxed{[N]·[M] = [P] \text{ where } P_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik} M_{kj} \text{ for } i,j \in \{1,2,3\}}

Confidence: 100%
extracted_answer: [N]·[M] = [P] \text{ where } P_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik} M_{kj} \text{ for } i,j \in \{1,2,3\}
